name: AWS API Gateway
what_it_does: AWS managed API gateway for creating, deploying, and managing REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs with Lambda integration, auth, throttling, and usage plans.
use_cases: Exposing AWS Lambda functions as HTTP endpoints for agent action APIs
WebSocket APIs for bidirectional real-time agent communication
Rate limiting and usage plans for multi-tenant agent API tiers
JWT/Cognito authorization at the gateway layer before Lambda invocation
Request/response transformation and mapping templates for legacy backend integration
not_for: Non-AWS deployments — deeply tied to IAM, Lambda, and AWS ecosystem
Complex API versioning strategies better handled by Kong or Apigee
Teams wanting simple API gateway without AWS account complexity
best_when: Your backend is AWS Lambda and you need managed routing, auth, throttling, and monitoring without running your own API server.
avoid_when: You're not on AWS or need advanced API management features like GraphQL federation, developer portals, or complex transformations.

auth.notes: IAM auth, Cognito User Pools, Lambda authorizers (custom), or API keys. HTTP API supports JWT authorizers natively. REST API requires Lambda authorizer for JWT.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1M HTTP API calls/month free for 12 months; 1M REST API calls/month free for 12 months
pricing.paid_tiers: HTTP API: $1.00/million requests; REST API: $3.50/million requests; WebSocket: $1.00/million messages
pricing.notes: HTTP API is 70% cheaper than REST API — prefer HTTP API for new projects unless you need REST API-specific features (usage plans, request validation).

performance.rate_limits: Default: 10K requests/second account-wide; configurable per stage

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Gateway-level errors (401, 403, 429, 500) return JSON with message field; Lambda errors wrapped in gateway response
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Gateway itself has no idempotency; idempotency is Lambda/backend responsibility
known_agent_gotchas: REST API vs HTTP API are completely different products — HTTP API is newer, cheaper, but lacks REST API features (API keys, usage plans, request validation)
Lambda proxy integration passes raw event — response must include statusCode, headers, and body as JSON string (not object)
Cold start latency adds to gateway latency — HTTP API + Lambda can spike to 500ms+ on cold starts
CORS must be configured on API Gateway AND returned in Lambda response headers for custom domains
Stage variables not available in HTTP API — use environment variables on Lambda instead

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM-based auth is very strong; JWT auth requires proper validation config. Resource policies for VPC/cross-account access.
